customer.primaryUseCase: AI-assisted remote incident triage for field devices
customer.businessProblem: Support engineering teams are overwhelmed diagnosing device errors across 800+ distributed robot deployments. Median time to diagnosis is 4.5 hours. First-time fix rate is 58%. Teams are escalating too many incidents to senior engineers.
customer.desiredOutcome: Reduce median incident triage time to under 90 minutes. Improve first-time fix rate to 75%. Reduce senior engineer escalations by 30%.

discovery.currentProcess: Field technicians submit incident tickets via Zendesk with a device ID and error description. Support engineers pull telemetry data from the device dashboard, cross-reference maintenance logs, and diagnose manually. Complex cases escalated to senior engineers or product team.
discovery.usersAffected: 14 support engineers, 6 field technicians, 3 senior engineers
discovery.constraints: Device telemetry data is incomplete for older hardware generations. Field technicians have variable technical skill. AI must not create false confidence — reliability expectations are high. Safety-critical decisions must remain human-approved.
discovery.knownRisks: Incomplete telemetry on legacy hardware. Edge-case device behaviour not in training data. Field technician over-reliance on AI diagnosis. Alert fatigue from noisy telemetry. Reliability expectations create low tolerance for incorrect suggestions.
discovery.successDefinition: Median triage time under 90 minutes. First-time fix rate 75%. Senior engineer escalations down 30%. No safety-related incidents caused by AI mis-diagnosis.
discovery.buyerTeam: VP Engineering, Head of Customer Success, Head of Product

stakeholders.name: Priya Nair
concerns: Telemetry data gaps in legacy hardware
requiredActions: Map telemetry fields to failure modes
Validate AI suggestions against known cases

workflows.name: Incident Ticket Submission
workflows.description: Field technician or customer submits incident via Zendesk. Device ID and error description captured.

workflows.name: Telemetry Pull and Review
workflows.description: Support engineer pulls device telemetry from dashboard and reviews error logs for the preceding 24 hours.
painPoints: Dashboard is slow for large data volumes

workflows.name: Diagnosis and Fix Recommendation
workflows.description: Support engineer reviews maintenance logs and customer notes to diagnose root cause and recommend fix.
painPoints: Maintenance logs are in spreadsheets — slow to search
Historical incident patterns not easily accessible
failureModes: Repeated escalations for same issue type

workflows.name: Escalation to Senior Engineer
workflows.description: Complex or ambiguous incidents escalated to senior engineers or product team.
painPoints: Senior engineers spend 40% of time on escalations
Context not well captured for escalations

systems.name: Atlas Device Dashboard
systems.notes: Internal telemetry platform. REST API available. Legacy hardware (Gen 1, 2) has limited telemetry fields. Gen 3+ has full sensor coverage.

systems.name: Zendesk
systems.notes: Standard Zendesk API. Well-documented. Webhook support available for real-time integration.

systems.name: Maintenance Logs
systems.notes: Currently in Excel spreadsheets maintained per device. ~5 years of records. Inconsistent format across hardware generations. Migration to structured format needed.

systems.name: Technical Documentation
systems.notes: Confluence wiki. PDF manuals for each hardware model. Well-maintained for Gen 3+. Gaps for Gen 1 and 2.

dataSources.name: Device Telemetry
openQuestions: Which telemetry fields are available on Gen 1/2?
What is the data retention period?

dataSources.name: Maintenance Logs
openQuestions: Can formats be standardised before ingestion?

risks.title: Incomplete telemetry on legacy hardware
risks.description: Gen 1 and 2 devices have limited sensor coverage. AI diagnosis quality will be lower for ~35% of the fleet.
risks.mitigation: Segment analysis by hardware generation. Set lower confidence thresholds for legacy hardware. Surface data gap warnings in the UI. Prioritise Gen 3+ for pilot.
risks.escalationTrigger: AI confidence below threshold on more than 40% of legacy device incidents

risks.title: Alert fatigue from telemetry noise
risks.description: High volume of telemetry events may produce noisy AI suggestions, reducing trust and adoption.
risks.mitigation: Filter to high-signal events before AI processing. Show top 3 candidate diagnoses with confidence scores. Allow engineers to rate AI suggestions.
risks.escalationTrigger: Engineer rating of AI suggestions falls below 3/5 average

risks.title: Field technician over-reliance on AI diagnosis
risks.description: Field technicians may act on AI suggestions without adequate review, causing incorrect repairs.
risks.mitigation: AI suggestions are for support engineers only — not directly visible to field technicians. Engineers must confirm before dispatching fix.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any incorrect field repair traced to un-reviewed AI suggestion

risks.title: Maintenance logs unstructured and inconsistent
risks.description: Excel-based maintenance logs require significant cleanup before they can be used as training or retrieval data.
risks.mitigation: Data engineering sprint to normalise maintenance logs before pilot. Define structured schema. Assign ongoing ownership.
risks.escalationTrigger: Maintenance log processing error rate above 15%

risks.title: Edge-case device behaviour not in training data
risks.description: Rare failure modes may not have sufficient historical examples for reliable AI pattern matching.
risks.mitigation: Curate edge-case library with senior engineers. Flag low-precedent diagnoses explicitly. Route low-confidence cases to senior engineer review.
risks.escalationTrigger: Two or more identical incorrect diagnoses on rare failure mode

pilotPlan.objective: Validate AI-assisted diagnosis reduces median triage time and improves first-time fix rate on Gen 3 devices.
pilotPlan.scope: Gen 3 devices only. Support engineer-facing only — not visible to field technicians. Mechanical and sensor failure categories only (excludes firmware and connectivity).

successMetrics.name: Median incident triage time
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Zendesk ticket timestamps from open to resolution

successMetrics.name: First-time fix rate
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Zendesk reopen rate within 7 days of resolution

launchCriteria: Maintenance log normalisation complete for Gen 3 devices
Safety review of AI output scope approved by VP Engineering
rollbackCriteria: Any safety-related incident linked to AI suggestion
First-time fix rate drops below baseline during pilot
AI suggestion quality rating below 2.5/5 for 5 consecutive days
Telemetry API failure lasting more than 2 hours
pilotPlan.baselineMeasurement: 4 weeks of Zendesk data reviewed for triage time, fix rate, and escalation rate. Gen 3 devices only.
pilotPlan.targetOutcome: Median triage time under 90 minutes. First-time fix rate 75%. Senior escalations down 30%. AI suggestion rating above 3.5/5.
